extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR Tadej won by attacking 3km out on gravel section, dropped QUINTANA and ROGLIČ who salvaged third after early distancing.
tactical_patterns: Movistar sacrificed SOLER's breakaway stage win with 3km left to pace QUINTANA, revealing team hierarchy prioritizes GC over opportunistic wins.
QUINTANA attacked on gravel section catching LÓPEZ/HERRADA, showing Movistar targets unconventional surfaces for decisive moves rather than steep gradients.
ROGLIČ appeared dropped but salvaged podium through superior pacing on final 1km, suggesting deliberate tempo management versus explosive attacks.
BEVIN led entire climb for breakaway then let PESCADOR take KOM points, demonstrating EF's willingness to work without reward for positioning.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: At 20 years old won by attacking solo 3km out, showing Grand Tour winning capability years before expected breakthrough
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: negative: Lost 1:17 and race lead after commanding position, cracking completely on gravel while rivals attacked through it
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: negative: Visibly disgusted when ordered to wait for QUINTANA despite riding toward stage win, revealing internal team conflict
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Appeared dropped mid-climb but closed gap and sprinted for 4-second bonus, showing tactical discipline over panic
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: Successfully attacked on gravel with VALVERDE to catch leaders, demonstrating surface-specific tactical awareness
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: Premature attack 3km out brought chasers back to LÓPEZ, nearly sabotaging team strategy before correction
rider_signals.CHAVES Esteban: negative: Mechanical/crash required bike changes from teammates 40cm taller, lost contact during critical selection phase
